reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart mechanism (MTTR measures repair speed not reliability), cover mean-vs-distribution, recurrence, incident frequency/severity, and converge on the same core remedy: SLOs/error-budget burn, total impact-minutes, P90/P99, recurrence rate, and pager fatigue. Each has one genuinely distinct point the other lacks: A adds 'detection lag excluded from MTTR clock' (a real and actionable measurement blind spot, tied to its time-to-detect metric) and change failure rate; B adds 'survivorship/classification bias' (chronic degradations and near-misses never becoming incidents, hence invisible to MTTR), which is a subtly different and valuable framing. Both end with the same useful diagnostic (plot count vs total impact-minutes). The unique contributions roughly offset, and overall structure/actionability are comparable, so neither is clearly superior.
